---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# snippet <a href="https://christopherkenny.github.io/snippet/"><img src="man/figures/logo.png" align="right" height="134" alt="snippet website" /></a>


<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

`snippet` creates shareable, syntax-highlighted code images directly from your R session.
It uses [Typst](https://typst.app) for rendering, which can create `png`, `pdf`, or `svg` outputs.

## Installation

Install the development version from [GitHub](https://github.com/christopherkenny/snippet):

``` r
pak::pak('christopherkenny/snippet')
```

`snippet` also requires [Typst](https://typst.app) to be installed on your system.
If you have Quarto installed, it will use its bundled Typst as a fallback.
See [typr](https://christophertkenny.com/typr/) for more details on the fallback strategy.

## Usage

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
library(snippet)
```

Pass code as a string, a character vector, or a file path. Leave `code` empty to use your clipboard:

```{r example, eval = FALSE}
library(snippet)

snippet('fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)\nsummary(fit)')
```

```{r example-img, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
snippet(
  'fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)\nsummary(fit)',
  output_file = 'man/figures/README-example.png'
)
knitr::include_graphics('man/figures/README-example.png')
```

### Themes

Two [Flexoki](https://stephango.com/flexoki) themes are bundled. List available themes with `snippet_themes()`:

```{r themes, eval = FALSE}
snippet(
  'fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)',
  theme = 'Flexoki Dark'
)
```

```{r themes-img, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
snippet(
  'fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)',
  theme = 'Flexoki Dark',
  output_file = 'man/figures/README-themes.png'
)
knitr::include_graphics('man/figures/README-themes.png')
```

Install additional themes by name with `snippet_install_theme()`. See the full list with `snippet_known_themes()`:

```{r install-theme, eval = FALSE}
snippet_install_theme('Dracula')
snippet_install_theme('Tomorrow Night')

snippet('fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)', theme = 'Dracula')
```

You can also install any theme from a URL or local file path:

```{r install-theme-url, eval = FALSE}
snippet_install_theme('https://example.com/my-theme.tmTheme')
snippet_install_theme('/path/to/my-theme.tmTheme')
```

### Window styles

The package supports Windows (default) and Mac browser controls.

```{r styles, eval = FALSE}
snippet('x <- 1', style = 'mac')
```

```{r styles-img, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
snippet(
  'x <- 1',
  style = 'mac',
  output_file = 'man/figures/README-styles-mac.png'
)
knitr::include_graphics('man/figures/README-styles-mac.png')
```

### Line numbers and width

```{r extras, eval = FALSE}
snippet(
  'x <- 1:10\nmean(x)\nsd(x)',
  line_numbers = TRUE,
  width = 4
)
```

```{r extras-img, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
snippet(
  'x <- 1:10\nmean(x)\nsd(x)',
  line_numbers = TRUE,
  width = 4,
  output_file = 'man/figures/README-extras.png'
)
knitr::include_graphics('man/figures/README-extras.png')
```
